channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La muerte de un inmigrante en Houston aviva el debate sobre la estrategia de ICE
item.description: La muerte a tiros de Lorenzo Salgado Araujo se produjo en un momento en el que se han intensificado las medidas de control de inmigración en todo el país, con miles de detenciones diarias.
item.dc:creator: Jazmine Ulloa, Hamed Aleaziz and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:description: La representante Sylvia Garcia habló durante una rueda de prensa sobre el caso de Lorenzo Salgado Araujo en Houston el viernes.

item.title: Developer of Troubled Midtown High-Rise Had Tried to Avoid the Spotlight
item.description: Nathan Berman has shied away from the showy ways necessary to succeed in New York real estate. Now, his largest project is in limbo.
item.dc:creator: Debra Kamin and Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:description: Nathan Berman has built his reputation as a real estate developer converting commercial buildings to luxury residences.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 11, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How ‘The Wire’ Star Jamie Hector Spends a Hot Day in Brooklyn
item.description: Bike rides, ice cream, museum, dinner with his wife: Mr. Hector’s busy day even included helping the next generation of actors.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Abrams

media:content.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Citizen Tests Positive for Ebola in Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The American works for a humanitarian organization in Congo, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Ebola outbreak has killed hundreds of people in the Democratic Republic of Congo this year.

item.title: Randolph Mantooth, Who Played John Gage in ‘Emergency!,’ Dies at 80
item.description: His portrayal of the firefighter and paramedic in the 1970s television hit helped expand the American public’s familiarity with emergency medicine.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon

media:content.media:description: Randolph Mantooth had an extensive career as an actor on television and the stage.

item.title: Reseña de ‘Moana’: un remake que pierde el rumbo
item.description: Disney vuelve sobre uno de sus mayores éxitos, pero esta versión de acción real ofrece muy poco que justifique su existencia.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: ¿Qué planes tiene Trump para Irán después del fracaso de la tregua?
item.description: A menos de un mes de la firma del acuerdo provisional, una serie de ataques contra tres barcos que atravesaban el estrecho de Ormuz llevaron a Trump a revocar la exención que permitía a Irán vender petróleo.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump en la Casa Blanca el lunes. Estados Unidos ha bombardeado más de 170 objetivos militares iraníes durante dos noches esta semana, tras el colapso del alto al fuego.

item.title: Times Journalists Subpoenaed as Trump Escalates Pressure on Media
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel testimony from reporters who wrote about the new Air Force One. The Times called the move a “brazen act.”
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporters before boarding the new Air Force One last week.

item.title: ‘They Don’t Need People’: The Workers Left Behind by China’s Robot Drive
item.description: For out-of-work factory workers in Kunshan, a region made rich by electronics manufacturing, a park is the only place to go.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang

media:content.media:description: Men looking for daily gigs in Kunshan, the heart of China’s manufacturing industry. As the country pivots toward automation, lower-skilled workers are finding fewer steady opportunities.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Estonia Beat Fentanyl. Next Scourge Was Worse.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, July 11, 2026.

item.title: Book Review: ‘Fixer Chao,’ by Han Ong
item.description: Han Ong’s barbed, irreverent portrait of identity, class envy and flimflam deception in turn-of-the-21st-century Manhattan still resonates.
item.dc:creator: Leah Greenblatt

item.title: What’s Kraken?
item.description: Dive into Jim Quinlan’s swell new themeless puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: A moray eel at the Neptune Memorial Reef, a man-made aquatic preserve and columbarium that holds the interred ashes of Julia Child, among others.

item.title: U.S. Employers Told to Dismiss Thousands of Immigrant Workers
item.description: Shifting deadlines are confusing businesses as the end of Temporary Protected Status for Haitians and others looms, making them ineligible to live and work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan and Madeleine Ngo

media:content.media:description: A rally in San Diego on Thursday in support of immigrants with Temporary Protected Status.

item.title: ‘Giulia’ Review: Jennifer Nettles Delivers Empowerment (and Poison)
item.description: A stirring but tonally muddled new musical about the “poison queen of Palermo” gets an elegant Off Broadway production.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: Perelman, Ronald O, Performing Arts Center
Giulia: The Poison Queen of Palermo (Play)

media:content.media:credit: Richard Termine for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jennifer Nettles as the title character in “Giulia: The Poison Queen of Palermo” at the Perelman Performing Arts Center in Manhattan.

item.title: Meta Removes A.I. Feature on Instagram After Days of Backlash
item.description: Users and Hollywood agencies raised privacy and copyright concerns about the new tool, Muse Image.
item.dc:creator: Eli Tan

item.title: After Being Delayed by Trump, a Canada-U.S. Bridge is Set to Open
item.description: A deal ended the Trump administration’s blocking of a new bridge that will ease congestion at the busiest trade corridor between the United States and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:description: The Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, in February.

item.title: Iranians Bury Slain Leader Amid Renewed Fighting
item.description: Iranians mourning the country’s supreme leader condemned U.S. strikes that Washington called retaliation for Iran’s attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. At a tightly controlled state funeral in Mashhad — one of Iran’s most conservative cities, where opponents of the government were unlikely to be found in the crowd — mourners voiced defiance and called for revenge.
item.dc:creator: Michael Anthony Adams, Mikhail Galustov, Jon Hazell, Artemis Moshtaghian and Jackeline Luna

item.title: D.S.A. Members Blast Mamdani Adviser for Elevating Graham Platner
item.description: A letter circulated by Democratic Socialists of America members asked that candidates sever ties with Morris Katz, a consultant for Mr. Platner and Mayor Zohran Mamdani.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Morris Katz, a political strategist who was a leader of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s campaign, is being criticized for his work with Graham Platner in Maine.

item.title: Historian Kerri K. Greenidge and Her Book ‘The Grimkes’ Come Under Fire
item.description: Kerri K. Greenidge appeared to lose her professorship at Tufts University after scholars began scrutinizing her 2022 book, “The Grimkes,” which is no longer listed on its publisher’s website.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.category: Black Radical: The Life and Times of William Monroe Trotter (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Mark Elzey Jr for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Historians are questioning the validity of multiple works by the historian Kerri K. Greenidge, who is no longer employed by Tufts University.

item.title: Ten Killed in Plane Crash on the Bahamas’ Largest Island
item.description: The crash occurred as the country celebrated its independence from Britain.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai

item.title: Buckling Manhattan High Rise Becomes Focus of Criminal Investigation
item.description: The inquiry, being conducted by the New York City Department of Investigation and the Manhattan district attorney’s office, was in an early stage, and its focus was unclear.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Hurubie Meko, Claire Fahy and Mihir Zaveri

media:content.media:description: Workers were converting an office building on East 42nd Street in Manhattan to apartments when they noticed bending steel and cracked concrete, prompting evacuations.

item.title: China Launches Reusable Rocket in Race With SpaceX
item.description: Video released by Chinese state media shows a state-owned aerospace company launching a rocket and recovering part of it on Friday. The successful launch of a reusable rocket was a major step for China toward challenging SpaceX’s satellite internet dominance.
item.dc:creator: David Cheung

item.title: New York Times Accuses Federal Employment Agency of Retaliation
item.description: A May lawsuit by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission claims the newspaper discriminated against a white male employee who did not get a promotion.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York Times filed a counterclaim against the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission in federal court on Friday.

item.title: Energy Abundance Is Here. Can the U.S. Catch Up?
item.description: The green energy transition is fully underway in countries like Australia and China, the environmentalist Bill McKibben explains. Can the U.S. catch up? He talks about his latest book, “Here Comes the Sun: A Last Chance for the Climate and a Fresh Chance for Civilization,” on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Green Tech’s ‘Small Miracles’
item.description: Green technology advancements in everything from water desalination to vertical farming are making it possible to tackle previously intractable environmental challenges. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” Bill McKibben, a founder of the environmental groups 350.org and Third Act, shares some of the success stories he’s seen through his work.

item.title: Charlie Cunningham, Mountain Bike Innovator, Dies at 77
item.description: In the late 1970s, he built what is considered the first off-road bicycle with a frame that was aluminum rather than steel, one of his many inventions.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

item.title: Mango Soft Serve
item.description: When an ice cream craving hits at home, you’ll find near-instant joy in this quick, fruity mango soft serve. Juicy, creamy, tangy or anywhere in between, any frozen fruit will work here; strawberry, peaches, raspberries and blueberries are also excellent choices.

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne.

item.title: N.Y.C. Official Planned Unauthorized Meeting With Iran’s U.N. Ambassador
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said the proposed meeting involving the mayor’s commissioner for international affairs, Ana María Archila, and the Iranian official had not taken place.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ana María Archila, the head of the Mayor’s Office for International Affairs in New York City, does not have significant diplomatic experience.

item.title: Trump Promotes ‘Freedom Fuel’ Gas Stations as Gas Prices Rise Again
item.description: President Trump has promoted a chain of newly rebranded gas stations across the Philadelphia area with lower gas prices. The New York Times has not been able to get detailed information about who is behind the stations. The Trump administration says it did not fund or subsidize the company.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A former Valero station is one in a chain of 25 newly branded Freedom Fuel gas stations in the Philadelphia region, promoted by President Trump and the White House.

item.title: What China’s Successful Rocket Launch Means for the Future of the Space Race
item.description: A space neophyte not long ago, China is now the United States’s main competitor for supremacy throughout the solar system.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:description: An image released by the Chinese state-run Xinhua News Agency purporting to show the returning first stage of the Long March 10B rocket on a seaborne platform in the South China Sea on Friday.

item.title: Dutch Museum Spreads 800 Pounds of Peanut Butter on the Floor to Honor a Late Artist
item.description: The Boijmans Van Beuningen Museum in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, unveiled an exhibit on Thursday featuring a 270-square-foot hexagon made of peanut butter. The installation was a tribute to Wim T. Schippers, the artist who first conceptualized the project and who died last month at 83.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:description: Workers at the Museum Boijmans Van Beuningen in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, recreated “Peanut Butter Floor,” an art installation by Wim T. Schippers, last week.

item.title: F.C.C. Approves Test of Space Mirror to Light Night Sky Despite Outcry
item.description: A start-up company has permission to try its plan to bounce solar rays onto the dark side of Earth, turning night to day for a three-mile-wide patch.
item.dc:creator: Hiroko Tabuchi

media:content.media:description: An artist’s concept of space mirrors in orbit.

item.title: Witnesses to ICE Killing Dispute the Official Account
item.description: Also, a major housing bill could become law without Trump’s signature. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The site where a federal immigration officer shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston.

item.title: Easy Chicken Tacos
item.description: Tender chicken tacos using boneless, skinless thighs make a flavorful weeknight meal that, if you warm up your tortillas while the chicken cooks, will be ready in about half an hour.

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.

item.title: Green Smoothie
item.description: For a green smoothie that’s neither too sweet nor reminiscent of gazpacho, make the “Stew” from Veggies Natural Juice Bar & Cafe in Brooklyn.

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne. Prop Stylist: Megan Hedgpeth.

item.title: Flooding in Missouri Prompts State of Emergency and at Least 100 Rescues
item.description: Gov. Mike Kehoe declared a state of emergency after up to a foot of rain fell overnight in the southeastern part of the state. More storms are in the forecast.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald and Amy Graff

item.title: The Platner Drama Is Reviving Debate Among Democrats Over a Double Standard
item.description: His candidacy stoked tensions among Democrats over gender and how to win back working-class voters.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:description: Fliers for the Platner campaign, which was suspended this week.

item.title: Arrest Made in Connection to Former U.K. Politician’s Murder
item.description: British police on Friday arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former right-wing politician.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:description: Ann Widdecombe speaking at a council meeting in Southend-on-Sea, England, in 2022.

item.title: Mick Jagger Isn’t Sure He Ever Lets the World See the Real Him
item.description: David Marchese speaks with Mick Jagger, the lead singer of the Rolling Stones, about his storied career and the band’s newest album, “Foreign Tongues.”

item.title: As the World Cup Wraps in L.A., the 2028 Olympics Beckon
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of people took public transit to World Cup events in car-crazy Los Angeles, a hopeful sign for officials planning the Summer Games.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango
item.category: Los Angeles Organizing Committee for the Olympic and Paralympic Games 2028 (LA28)

media:content.media:description: Riders await a train along the K Line at the LAX Metro Transit Center in Los Angeles.

item.title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of Stealing Company Secrets
item.description: The two companies struck a deal in 2024 to offer A.I. services on Apple devices, but their partnership has soured.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Cade Metz

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Apple made several accusations against OpenAI in its lawsuit, including that OpenAI had asked job candidates from Apple to share details about secret projects.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Will Soon Be Declared Open to All Traffic, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The officials said that Iranian negotiators blamed the recent ship attacks on rogue military units.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo vessels anchored off the coast of Oman, just outside the Strait of Hormuz, last month.

item.title: Graham Platner Files Paperwork to Withdraw From Maine Senate Race
item.description: The move paved the way for Democrats to choose a new nominee to challenge Senator Susan Collins in a race both parties see as key to the control of the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner during his primary watch party last month.

item.title: México presentará denuncias penales en EE. UU. por las muertes de migrantes
item.description: La presidenta del país prometió ir “más allá de las notas diplomáticas” después de que un agente de inmigración estadounidense matara a Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, un ciudadano mexicano, en Texas esta semana.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una vigilia con velas el miércoles en Houston, en el lugar donde Lorenzo Salgado Araujo fue abatido el día anterior.

item.title: 5 European Hotels Where You Can Beat the Heat
item.description: Whether hidden in the mountains or nestled by the sea, these spots offer some respite from the continent’s high temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Adam H. Graham

item.title: ICE detuvo a un antiguo aliado de Pinochet. ¿Será deportado?
item.description: Armando Fernández Larios ayudó a planear un ataque con coche bomba en Washington que mató a dos personas en 1976. Cincuenta años después, antiguos secretos del gobierno podrían decidir su destino.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: Armando Fernández Larios en 2003. Durante años, el gobierno de Estados Unidos consideró a Fernández como una especie de aliado útil durante la Guerra Fría, incluso cuando su relación con Chile se deterioraba.

item.title: What Is the Best Verse on Jay-Z’s ‘Reasonable Doubt’?
item.description: Music critic and “Popcast” host, Jon Caramanica, makes his argument for why Jay-Z’s “Friend or Foe” is the best verse on his 1996 album “Reasonable Doubt.”
item.dc:creator: Popcast

item.title: N.Y.P.D. Inspector Charged with Attempted Rape of a Female Officer
item.description: Inspector Jeremy Scheublin, 45, is accused of trying to sexually assault an officer who worked in his precinct. He pleaded not guilty on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer and Miles G. Cohen

item.title: Judge Allows News Site to Publish School Lockdown Video but Sets Limits
item.description: Lawyers for the outlet, New Brunswick Today, said that the order, while a partial victory, still violated the First Amendment. They plan to appeal.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Haag
item.category: New Brunswick High School (New Brunswick, NJ)

media:content.media:description: Charlie Kratovil, editor of New Brunswick Today, said he did not plan to republish a video of a school lockdown after a judge imposed limits on its use.
